fund.name: Motilal Oswal Large and Midcap Fund - Direct Plan Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Top 5-year return (21.7% CAGR) in Large & Midcap category with highest Sharpe (1.22). Equity-taxed, ideal for aggressive growth. AMFI 147704.

fund.name: Invesco India Midcap Fund - Direct Plan - Growth Option

allocations.rationale: Exceptional 5-year returns (21.5% CAGR) with high Sharpe (1.15) and low tracking error (9.89%). Evidence-based mid-cap exposure. AMFI 120403.

fund.name: BANDHAN SMALL CAP FUND - DIRECT PLAN GROWTH

allocations.rationale: Highest 5-year return (23.7% CAGR) in small-cap category. Equity-taxed, adds diversification. AMFI 147946.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal S&P 500 Index Fund - Direct Plan Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: International diversification with ultra-low fee (0.2% ER). High Sharpe (2.65) and alpha (49%). Equity-taxed. AMFI 148381.
Gold ETF for diversification and inflation hedge. Slab-taxed (30%), but low correlation to equity improves portfolio Sharpe. AMFI 151737.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Kuvera, Groww, or MFU. Step 2: Start monthly SIPs: ₹45k in Motilal Oswal Large & Midcap, ₹37.5k in Invesco Midcap, ₹22.5k in Bandhan Small Cap, ₹22.5k in Motilal Oswal S&P 500, ₹15k in DSP Gold ETF. Step 3: Deploy lumpsum: ₹12L in Motilal Oswal Large & Midcap, ₹10L in Invesco Midcap, ₹6L in Bandhan Small Cap, ₹6L in Motilal Oswal S&P 500, ₹4L in DSP Gold ETF. Step 4: Set up step-up SIP of 10% annually.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Check if SIPs are running and lumpsum deployed.
After 1 year: Compare fund performance to category benchmarks; replace any fund underperforming by >3% for 2 consecutive quarters.
Every 2 years: Rebalance to original allocation percentages.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check if your mix has changed by >5% from target. If equity has grown to >85%, book profits and move to debt/gold. If equity falls to <70%, deploy surplus to equity. Use arbitrage funds for tax-efficient parking.
plan.rationale: Plan built for 34-year-old aggressive investor with 18-year horizon. Equity allocation 85% (Large & Midcap 30%, Midcap 25%, Smallcap 15%, International 15%), 10% gold, 0% debt (equity-taxed preferred over debt for 30% slab). Tax-efficient: all equity funds LTCG 12.5% after ₹1.25L exemption. Evidence-based selection: each fund top in category by 5-year returns and Sharpe. International diversification via S&P 500 index fund with low fee. Gold adds diversification with high Sharpe. Monthly SIP ₹142,500 matches investible surplus; lumpsum deployment uses existing corpus efficiently. Goal: 12Cr portfolio by 52 via 12.8% base CAGR, compounded from ₹40L corpus + ₹1.71L monthly SIP for 18 years.
risks: Markets can drop 20-30% in a bad year—portfolio value will temporarily go down. This is normal and recovers over time.
Small-cap funds can be more volatile; expect larger swings.
International funds are subject to currency risk; rupee depreciation can boost returns but also add volatility.
Gold returns are slab-taxed (30%), reducing post-tax returns vs. equity.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

allocation_schedule.trigger: Rebalance towards safer assets as goal nears

aps.reasoning: Most of the equity allocation is to actively managed funds: Motilal Oswal Large & Midcap, Invesco Midcap, and Bandhan Small Cap together account for 70% of the portfolio and are high tracking error/high alpha funds. Only 15% is a passive instrument (Motilal Oswal S&P 500 Index Fund), so the passive_instrument_fraction is 0.15. The plan features annual rebalancing with additional review after 1 year and a 2-year replacement check if a fund underperforms, making turnover_score moderate at 0.5 (not a buy-and-hold, but not highly active turnover). There is some cost sensitivity (e.g., S&P 500 fund chosen for ultra-low fee; mention of tax efficiency), but cost is not the dominant factor; most funds have high expense ratios, so cost_emphasis_score is 0.4. Research_vs_cost_score is low (0.2): selection is explicitly based on category-beating 5-year returns and Sharpe, i.e., deep fund-specific quantitative research rather than broad index exposure. Time_horizon_alignment_score is maxed (1.0) due to the explicit 18-year horizon, SIP step-up, and advice to not worry about volatility, indicating a long compounding strategy. Portfolio_activeness_score is very low (0.1): equity funds are quantified as highly active (alphas well above 0, tracking errors above 10%, betas deviate from 1.0), so this is an 'active' equity portfolio with only a slight passive tilt from the S&P 500 slice and the Gold ETF (which, although an ETF, is not equity and is slab-taxed, so doesn't add much to equity passiveness).

pqs.reasoning: This plan demonstrates exceptional alignment with the investor profile: the aggressive equity allocation, step-up SIP, and evidence-driven fund selection fit Sheldon's 18-year, high-return, Sharpe-focused objectives. The projected returns (12.8% base, 16.8% bull, 8.8% bear) are ambitious but credible for a long horizon and aggressive equity stance. Diversification is strong (large/mid/small cap, US equities, gold, multiple AMCs, and both active and passive styles) but could improve with a wider range of AMCs or inclusion of a dedicated flexicap or emerging market international fund—hence 0.9 not 1.0. The rationale for each allocation is fully evidence-based with clear quantitative backup matching the stated preferences. Risks, rebalancing, and review triggers are all precisely articulated and internally coherent. Tax is treated thoughtfully: the rationale prefers equity-taxed funds over debt for the 30% slab, highlights the slab-taxed gold drag, and even mentions using arbitrage funds for tax-efficient parking when booking profits from equities—though this isn't explicit in the initial allocation. It could be even more tax-optimal if it justified (or dismissed) the absence of ELSS for 80C, or specifically included a short note on why international equity via fund-of-funds is not being penalized (they're still equity-taxed if underlying equity ≥65%). However, this is factually accurate for all funds listed. In sum, the plan is highly professional, quantitative, and leaves little unaddressed; minor points keep it shy of a perfect 1.0 in some areas, but scores are all very high.
